summary:
The upper jaw is normally positioned while the lower jaw appears more retracted; this results from typical characteristics described under Moyer’s Class II Type D category for malocclusions.
The patient exhibits features consistent with Moyer’s Class II Type D, indicating a relatively well-positioned maxilla combined with a recessive mandibular position. \boxed{C}
